summary: Iteration 2 testing of the archive.org audio integration. Backend: 18/18 pytest tests pass — covers /api/catalog, new /api/music-wellness/generate (Node contract: validation envelope {error, message}, intensity buckets, search query + tags, archive.org tracks), legacy /api/playlist (now also archive.org with SoundHelix fallback, exposes both `url` and `audioUrl` fields), and parse_length_to_seconds helper parity. Frontend: full happy-path wizard works end-to-end and renders REAL archive.org content ('Various - Clinical Sounds - Vol.1 (ca015)', audio src = https://archive.org/download/ca015_va_cs/...mp3), queue shows 2 tracks with title+artist, exit → feedback → completion all functional. No critical issues found.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: deprecated @app.on_event('shutdown') still in place — migrate to FastAPI lifespan context manager (non-blocking; carried over from iteration 1).
server.py CORS: allow_credentials=True with allow_origins='*' is technically invalid per spec; currently OK since no credentialed requests are made.

audio_archive_service.py: fetch_wellness_tracks caches empty lists for 30 minutes when archive.org search fails — this can extend an outage past recovery. Consider caching only non-empty results (cache.set is called regardless after the try/except returns []).
audio_archive_service.py: AsyncClient is created per call rather than reused; for higher load a shared httpx.AsyncClient (with proper shutdown) would reduce TLS handshake overhead.

server.py: _generate_tracks rounds n = round(session_duration/5) but clamps to [2,6]; for sessionDuration=5 → n=1 → clamped to 2 (fine), but worth a code comment so the next reader understands the intent.
Frontend Soundlull.jsx: error toast still has no retry button — user must reload if /api/catalog fails on first load (carried over from iteration 1).

success_rate.frontend: 100% happy-path passed end-to-end with real archive.org track playing

test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: None — catalog is static; tracks come from archive.org with SoundHelix fallback.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file: /app/backend/tests/test_soundlull_api.py — covers all three endpoints plus parse_length_to_seconds. Validation contract is {error: 'ValidationError', message: <str>} (NOT FastAPI's default {detail:...}); this is enforced by a custom @app.exception_handler(HTTPException) in server.py — preserve it. Intensity bucket logic: 1-2 → 'low', 3-5 → 'high'. /api/playlist exposes BOTH `url` (for legacy AudioPlayer.jsx which reads track.url) and `audioUrl` (new). Archive.org calls are cached 30 min; first call can take 2-5s — pytest timeouts set to 30s. Frontend wizard data-testids confirmed working (same as iteration 1): welcome-begin-button, mood-card-{id}, mood-next-button, intensity-option-{id}, intensity-next-button, duration-option-{N}, duration-start-button, audio-player-screen, current-track-title, queue-list, queue-item-{i}, player-exit-button, feedback-option-{id} (5 of them), feedback-submit-button, completion-screen, restart-session-button. Real archive.org track example observed: 'Various - Clinical Sounds - Vol.1 (ca015)' from https://archive.org/download/ca015_va_cs/101.Etat_Des_Stocks_Klassikkos.mp3.
